channel.title: US Top News and Analysis
channel.description: CNBC is the world leader in business news and real-time financial market coverage. Find fast, actionable information.

item.title: Brent crude rises 2% as U.S. military strikes against Iran cloud Middle East peace prospects
item.description: Donald Trump had said negotiations with Iran were "proceeding nicely," while warning that the U.S. could resume military action if discussions were to collapse.

item.title: U.S. conducts 'self-defense strikes' in Iran as Trump pushes for peace deal
item.description: Targets included missile launch sites and Iranian boats attempting to emplace mines.

item.title: Ferrari shares fall 6% after luxury carmaker launches first fully electric vehicle
item.description: Shares of luxury carmaker Ferrari fell sharply on Tuesday morning, shortly after the company launched its first fully electric vehicle.

item.title: Russia tells Marco Rubio U.S. citizens should leave Kyiv ahead of 'systematic' strikes on Ukrainian capital
item.description: Moscow said it would be launching “systematic and consistent strikes” on the city of Kyiv.

item.title: These tech stocks have seen bullish 'flag patterns' recently, says Katie Stockton
item.description: The founder of Fairlead Strategies breaks down how this chart formation occurs.

item.title: Iran is in the 'process of blinking' over the Strait of Hormuz, Petraeus says
item.description: Ex-CIA director David Petraeus said that an initial successful peace deal with Tehran would see the Strait opened without any conditions.

item.title: Ferrari's electric ambitions with the Luce launch
item.description: Ferrari has unveiled its first fully electric car, co-designed by former Apple chief designer Jony Ive. The 'Ferrari Luce' is a five-seater, powered by an electric engine on each wheel - and is able to reach 100 kilometres per hour in just 2-and-a-half seconds. CNBC's Charlotte Reed speaks to the CEO, Benedetto Vigna.

item.title: European stocks edge lower amid U.S-Iran peace talks uncertainty
item.description: European stocks edged lower Tuesday, as traders watched developments in the Middle East and Ukraine.

item.title: EU plans to fine Google high triple-digit million euro sum, Handelsblatt reports
item.description: The fine would be the largest penalty the EU has imposed for a breach of its Digital Markets Act, which aims to curb the power of big tech companies.

item.title: China tech plays to ride out macro volatility
item.description: The China stock play right now is to focus on artificial intelligence-related names, regardless of the slower economic growth, several analysts said.

item.title: Boosting retirement savings has a less-appreciated benefit
item.description: Saving more money has a dual benefit that often goes unnoticed, according to financial advisors.

item.title: Americans are 'entrenched' in financial stress amid debt and price pressures
item.description: Inflation and high debt levels continue cause financial anxiety for many Americans. Nonprofits can help with debt mangement.

item.title: CNBC's The China Connection newsletter: Tariffs eased. Trust didn't.
item.description: As the largest consumer market globally, the U.S. still remains a prized destination for Chinese companies that are finding ways to address local concerns.

item.title: Want to hop off the AI trade? Goldman says buy these stocks that have nothing to do with it
item.description: Stocks such as Eli Lilly and Fortinet are less correlated to the artificial intelligence frenzy, according to Goldman Sachs.

item.title: South Korea's Kospi hits new high amid mixed trading in Asia
item.description: Asia-Pacific markets were set to open broadly higher Tuesday, amid hopes for a breakthrough in the U.S.-Iran peace negotiations.

item.title: Oil market at 'tank bottoms' in Asia, and Europe isn't far behind, warns market veteran Jeff Currie
item.description: Oil markets are nearing minimum operating levels in Asia, with Europe likely next and the U.S. potentially facing shortages by July, said Carlyle's Jeff Currie.

item.title: Trump links Abraham Accords to Iran deal
item.description: Trump said he has asked countries including Qatar, Saudi Arabia, Pakistan, Egypt, Jordan and Turkey to join the Abraham Accords en masse.

item.title: The Middle East war is testing the Gulf's ambitions to become an AI hub
item.description: Attacks on data centers in the Middle East and persistently high energy prices have altered the calculus for operators of the facilities, analysts say.

item.title: Huawei plans new smartphone chips this fall as rivalry with Nvidia and Apple heats up
item.description: Chinese telecom giant Huawei announced Monday a new way of designing chips that improves their capabilities, despite U.S. restrictions on the company.

item.title: Iran and U.S. play down hopes for imminent breakthrough in war
item.description: Secretary of State Marco Rubio told reporters in New Delhi that the U.S. would give diplomacy every chance to succeed before exploring "alternatives".

item.title: It's time for Nvidia to take a page out of Apple's playbook and do more for investors
item.description: It's time for Nvidia to admit its status as a solid grower and do more for shareholders than it already has.

item.title: The 5 best affordable hotel brands for your next trip
item.description: We reviewed more than 2 dozen hotels to determine our top picks for affordable hotel brands across the U.S

item.title: Gold rises as dollar, oil ease on U.S.-Iran deal prospects
item.description: Spot gold was up 1.1% at $4,559.07 per ounce, as ​of 0736 GMT. U.S. gold futures for June delivery gained 0.8% ​to $4,559.80.

item.title: Pope Leo urges AI regulation and warns that some weapons are now beyond human control
item.description: The first U.S. pope called for ownership of AI data not to be left solely in private hands and for policy makers to protect the rights of workers and children.

item.title: When are prediction markets most helpful? Evercore ISI has a formula
item.description: Evercore ISI strategists provide insight for when prediction markets are useful for forecasting.

item.title: Delivery Hero shares rise 10% as rival Uber mulls another takeover bid
item.description: Delivery Hero confirmed in a statement on Saturday that it had received a takeover offer from Uber of €33 per share.

item.title: European stocks highest since March 2 as U.S.-Iran talks continue; euro zone bond yields drop on peace hopes
item.description: Stocks in Europe are tracking their Asian counterparts higher after Japan's Nikkei 225 breached 65,000 for the first time.

item.title: Singapore reports lower-than-expected inflation for April at 1.8%, revises economic growth higher
item.description: Core inflation — which strips out prices of private transport and accommodation — came in at 1.4%, against estimates of 1.7%.

item.title: Beware the boom and bust cycle of memory stocks, investors warn amid AI excitement
item.description: "In the long run, it's a pretty dreadful industry," William de Gale of BlueBox Asset Management, told CNBC's Europe Early Edition on Wednesday.

item.title: Japan’s Nikkei 225 tops 65,000 for first time as oil falls on Hormuz reopening hopes
item.description: Japan’s Nikkei 225 breached 65,000 for the first time Monday as a sharp decline in oil prices lifted risk sentiment in holiday-thinned trading.
